sentence: The company decided to phase ______ the old product line gradually.
explanation: El phrasal verb <phase out> significa eliminar gradualmente.
tip: The company is gradually eliminating the old product line.

sentence: We need to iron ______ the details before signing the contract.
explanation: El phrasal verb <iron out> significa resolver problemas o dificultades.
tip: Resolve the details — smooth out the problems.

sentence: The manager tried to play ______ the severity of the issue.
explanation: El phrasal verb <play down> significa minimizar la importancia de algo.
tip: The manager tried to minimize the severity.

sentence: She was passed ______ for promotion despite her qualifications.
explanation: El phrasal verb <pass over> significa ignorar a alguien para una oportunidad.
tip: She was ignored for the promotion — they skipped her.

sentence: The detective pieced ______ the evidence to solve the case.
explanation: El phrasal verb <piece together> significa reconstruir algo a partir de fragmentos.
tip: The detective reconstructed the evidence bit by bit.

sentence: They had to scale ______ operations due to budget cuts.
explanation: El phrasal verb <scale back> significa reducir el tamaño o alcance de algo.
tip: They reduced the size of operations due to budget cuts.

sentence: The new policy was brought ______ to improve efficiency.
explanation: El phrasal verb <bring in> significa introducir algo nuevo.
tip: A new policy was introduced to improve efficiency.

sentence: He was let ______ when the company downsized.
explanation: El phrasal verb <let go> significa despedir a alguien de forma diplomática.
tip: He was dismissed diplomatically when the company downsized.

sentence: The team rallied ______ their captain after the defeat.
explanation: El phrasal verb <rally around> significa unirse para apoyar a alguien.
tip: The team united to support their captain.

sentence: She stumbled ______ an interesting article while researching.
explanation: El phrasal verb <stumble upon> significa encontrar algo por casualidad.
tip: She found an article by accident while researching.

sentence: The negotiations fell ______ at the last minute.
explanation: El phrasal verb <fall through> significa fracasar o no concretarse.
tip: The negotiations failed — they didn't materialize.

sentence: We need to factor ______ the cost of transportation.
explanation: El phrasal verb <factor in> significa incluir algo en un cálculo o plan.
tip: Include the transportation cost in the calculation.

sentence: The CEO stepped ______ after the scandal.
explanation: El phrasal verb <step down> significa renunciar a un cargo.
tip: The CEO resigned from his position after the scandal.

sentence: They managed to fend ______ the hostile takeover.
explanation: El phrasal verb <fend off> significa defenderse de un ataque o amenaza.
tip: They defended against the hostile takeover attempt.

sentence: The project was held ______ by regulatory issues.
explanation: El phrasal verb <hold up> significa retrasar o demorar.
tip: The project was delayed by regulatory issues.

sentence: She opted ______ of the voluntary redundancy scheme.
explanation: El phrasal verb <opt out> significa decidir no participar.
tip: She decided not to participate in the scheme.

sentence: The committee drew ______ a list of recommendations.
explanation: El phrasal verb <draw up> significa preparar o redactar un documento.
tip: The committee prepared a list of recommendations.

sentence: He was singled ______ for his exceptional performance.
explanation: El phrasal verb <single out> significa elegir a alguien de un grupo.
tip: He was chosen from the group for his performance.

sentence: The reforms were watered ______ to gain political support.
explanation: El phrasal verb <water down> significa debilitar o suavizar algo.
tip: The reforms were weakened to gain political support.

sentence: They need to hammer ______ the final agreement.
explanation: El phrasal verb <hammer out> significa negociar intensamente hasta llegar a un acuerdo.
tip: They negotiated intensely to reach the final agreement.

sentence: The old regulations were done ______ with last year.
explanation: El phrasal verb <do away with> significa abolir o eliminar.
tip: The old regulations were abolished last year.

sentence: She was taken ______ by the unexpected generosity.
explanation: El phrasal verb <take aback> significa sorprender o desconcertar.
tip: She was surprised by the unexpected generosity.

sentence: The company branched ______ into new markets.
explanation: El phrasal verb <branch out> significa expandirse a nuevas áreas.
tip: The company expanded into new markets.

sentence: He glossed ______ the controversial aspects of the report.
explanation: El phrasal verb <gloss over> significa tratar superficialmente algo problemático.
tip: He treated the controversial aspects superficially.

sentence: The team pulled ______ a remarkable victory.
explanation: El phrasal verb <pull off> significa lograr algo difícil.
tip: The team achieved a remarkable victory against the odds.
